repo_prior_art.cite: none exact; closest API PartialID/Manski bounds and cited pid_* proposals do not state deterministic heap-map aliasing for bunching mass

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMoment + ManskiTamer2002IntervalData: generic convex/interval partial-identification support machinery

repo_prior_art.cite: none exact; pid_multivariate_interval_regression is only an adjacent failed endpoint-pruning warning and has no matching §8 result

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: rho_b(A,B,r,M) is defined as the dimension of a set with the constraint b^T h != 0, which is not a vector space; the phase threshold is therefore not mathematically defined.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The point-identification half relies on 'remaining side-stencil restrictions point identify' but §7 does not assume those restrictions eliminate all nonlocal or side-stencil null directions.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The endpoint replacement rule E_j and the endpoint-pruning frontier are not defined in §6 or §7, so Assumption 6 and the conjecture quantify over an undefined operator.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine rho_b as a genuine rank, for example dim L - dim(L ∩ ker b^T) or the rank of b^T restricted to the local heap-null tangent space, then restate the zero/positive phase clauses with that object.

Add an explicit side-stencil completeness or row-span condition under which all feasible target-moving directions are exhausted by the rho_b space; otherwise weaken the zero-phase clause to a local no-aliasing statement.

Define the endpoint operator E_j and the induced endpoint-pruned identified set before Assumption 6, then restate Conjecture 2 as equality or strict containment between that set and the true heap-aliasing frontier.

recommended_next_step: Patch the two undefined objects and add the missing side-stencil identification condition; the novelty kernel survives if those repairs are made.
